title: Verilator doesn't like `$bits(inst.typedef_t)` hierarchical reference.
body:
Hi there,

I've actually been trying to solve an Xcelium issue in some Renode code, discussion [here](https://github.com/renode/renode/issues/619), and in fixing Xcelium, I'm now finding that Verilator barfs.

The original issue was due to a non-LRM compliant use of a hierarchical dot path to a parameter used in a constant expression.  The Xcelium workaround is to use `[$bits(interface.data_t)-1:0]` instead of `[interface.DataWidth-1:0]`, where `data_t` is defined in the interface using the parameter and Verilator doesn't like that syntax.

I've included a minimal testcase that uses the Renode code.

Verilator version: Verilator 5.024 2024-04-05 rev v5.024
OS: Rocky Linux 9.4

[testcase.zip](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/15408923/testcase.zip)

Oh, and the "zip" file is a "tgz" to get around the upload restrictions.
